title: A successful CalFresh program has relaunched in November to make healthy fruits and vegetables more affordable. With $10 million in new state funding
prompt_preview:

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The CalFresh Fruit and Vegetable EBT Pilot Project has been relaunched with $10 million in new state funding.",
    "The program has over 90 grocery stores across California participating.",
    "Each $1 of CalFresh benefits spent on fresh fruits and vegetables provides CalFresh recipients $1 back to spend on any CalFresh-eligible food.",
    "The CalFresh Fruit and Vegetable Pilot has served at least 93,000 households and provided $10.5 million in rebates.",
    "Over 20% of families in California are experiencing food insecurity.",
    "The annual health care costs associated with food insecurity in California total more than $7 billion.",
    "The CalFresh Fruit and Vegetable Pilot helps families make ends meet with extra CalFresh benefits.",
    "Having access to fresh, nutritious food can help prevent serious illnesses.",
    "The Pilot Project allows CalFresh participants to choose healthy foods for their families.",
    "Having a healthy, well-rounded diet will result in better health outcomes for CalFresh participants."
  ]
}
```
